extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR won the Stage 5 ITT by 19 seconds over KÜNG, but VAN DER POEL defended yellow by just 8 seconds after a desperate finishing sprint.
tactical_patterns: BJERG allowed free ride by UAE as POGAČAR protected, went 53s clear early suggesting team hierarchies fluid pre-ITT
VAN DER POEL needed 32:38 split, came in at exactly threshold showing precise pacing execution under extreme pressure
CATTANEO 6s faster than BJERG at checkpoint 1 before fading reveals early checkpoint speed doesn't correlate to TT finish
Weather window timing critical: early starters caught rain, final 45 riders benefited from dry roads creating 50s+ swing

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Never paused on time checks, put time into every rider throughout suggests ITT dominance sustainable all Tour
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: Retained yellow by 8s despite doubting himself pre-race shows mental barriers less limiting than physical
rider_signals.BJERG Mikkel: positive: Given team freedom and finished 53s clear of early starters indicates UAE willing to sacrifice ITT stage for GC
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: Second at 19s behind POGAČAR confirms elite TT status but reveals gap to very best
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Dropped to 48s back from 8s deficit shows ITT weakness more severe than expected for former World Champion
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: At 1:48 back reveals surprising time loss for specialist, suggests injury or form concerns emerging early
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: 1:44 deficit confirms ITT as major GC vulnerability, will need mountain aggression to compensate
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Strong result mentioned alongside top finishers suggests ITT form carrying from spring classics
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: Just under minute back shows solid but unspectacular ITT form for veteran GC rider
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: Virtual rest day approach confirms sprinters treating ITT as recovery between flat stages
